disclaimer: ILLUSTRATIVE / SYNTHETIC. These career paths are hand-written for the Teacher Copilot demo to be realistic for the Indian context. They are NOT scraped job-market data and contain no salary figures or guarantees. Verify specifics before relying on them.

paths.title: EdTech Content Developer
paths.description: Create lessons, question banks, explainer scripts and learning material for edtech platforms and apps.
skills_to_build: familiarity with a platform's style guide
paths.indian_context_notes: Edtech content roles strongly value NCERT/CBSE familiarity and the ability to write for both English and regional-language learners. A small portfolio of sample lessons matters more than a certificate.
example_next_steps: make 3-4 polished sample lessons in your subject
learn a content tool (Google Docs style guides, basic Canva)
apply to content teams at edtech companies and freelance marketplaces

paths.title: Instructional Designer
paths.description: Design how courses are structured and sequenced so learners actually learn — objectives, activities, assessments, and flow.
skills_to_build: an authoring tool (Articulate, H5P, or similar)
paths.indian_context_notes: Growing demand from edtech firms, corporate L&D, and universities going online. Teaching experience is a real asset because you understand how students struggle.
example_next_steps: take a free/low-cost ID course
redesign one of your own units using backward design
build a portfolio storyboard and one interactive module

paths.title: Curriculum Developer
paths.description: Plan and write curriculum, scope-and-sequence documents, and teacher guides for schools, boards, or publishers.
paths.indian_context_notes: Publishers and school chains hire for this; deep knowledge of a specific board's framework (CBSE/ICSE/state) and NEP 2020 directions is valued.
example_next_steps: study your board's framework end-to-end
draft a sample scope-and-sequence for one grade
reach out to educational publishers and school groups

paths.title: Corporate L&D / Trainer
paths.description: Train adult employees in companies — onboarding, soft skills, communication, and domain topics.
paths.indian_context_notes: IT/BPO/BFSI sectors hire trainers heavily. Communication and soft-skills training is a common first entry point for teachers.
example_next_steps: run a couple of free workshops to build a reel/testimonials
learn a facilitation framework
apply to L&D teams and training vendors

paths.title: Test-Prep / Coaching Educator
paths.description: Teach competitive-exam or board-exam prep (JEE/NEET/UPSC foundations, boards) at coaching institutes or online.
typical_transition_from: subject teacher with strong exam results
paths.indian_context_notes: A large, established market. Track record (student results) is the currency. Online coaching has widened where you can teach from.
example_next_steps: specialise in one exam and one subject
record a few sample problem walkthroughs
join an established institute or build an online batch

paths.title: Educational Content Creator
paths.description: Build your own teaching brand via YouTube, short videos, or self-published courses.
paths.indian_context_notes: Regional-language explainer content has huge underserved demand. Income is uneven and builds slowly — usually a side path before it's a main one.
example_next_steps: publish 10 short explainers on one topic
learn basic editing
engage with your comments to build a small audience

paths.title: Academic Coordinator / School Leadership
paths.description: Move into leading a subject department, coordinating academics, or eventually school administration.
paths.indian_context_notes: A natural in-school growth path. Some schools value a B.Ed./M.Ed. or leadership certification for formal promotion.
example_next_steps: volunteer to coordinate a school initiative
mentor a junior teacher
discuss a coordinator track with your principal

paths.title: Assessment / Item Writer
paths.description: Write high-quality exam questions, rubrics, and assessments for boards, edtech, and test-prep companies.
typical_transition_from: teacher who enjoys setting and marking papers
paths.indian_context_notes: Edtech and assessment firms need reliable item writers, often freelance to start. Familiarity with board marking schemes helps.
example_next_steps: write a calibrated question set with rubrics for one unit
study a released board marking scheme closely
apply to assessment teams for freelance trials

paths.title: Teacher Trainer / Teacher Educator
paths.description: Train and coach other teachers — pedagogy workshops, in-service training, and B.Ed. teaching.
typical_transition_from: experienced teacher with strong classroom craft
paths.indian_context_notes: NGOs, government programmes, school chains, and B.Ed. colleges all hire teacher trainers. Field credibility from real classroom years matters a lot.
example_next_steps: design and run one teacher workshop
learn to give observation feedback
connect with education NGOs and training programmes

paths.title: EdTech Academic Specialist / Product
paths.description: Bridge teaching and product teams at an edtech company — advising on pedagogy, reviewing content, and shaping learning features.
paths.indian_context_notes: These roles reward teachers who can speak both 'classroom' and 'product'. Often reachable after a stint in edtech content or ID.
example_next_steps: get in via a content/ID role first
learn how product teams work
volunteer for pedagogy reviews of features
